name: Dify
tagline: 'Apache 2.0' — with conditions that break the promise
description: A production-oriented platform for agentic workflows and LLM apps: a visual workflow editor, RAG pipeline, agent framework, model management, and observability in one self-hostable package.
why: Hugely popular and routinely labeled 'Apache 2.0' — but it's Apache 2.0 with additional conditions, and those conditions (no multi-tenant operation without authorization; no removing the Dify logo or copyright) impose field-of-use restrictions that fail the Open Source Definition.
audience: Teams wanting an all-in-one self-hosted LLMOps platform

setup.requirements: Docker Compose multi-service stack; ~4GB+ RAM; provider keys

license.code: Apache-2.0 with additional conditions (source-available, not OSI)
license.notes: Describe it as source-available or open-core — never plain 'Apache 2.0'.

limitations: The multi-tenant and branding conditions are exactly the kind of use restrictions the open-source definition rules out.
take: The most common licensing sleight-of-hand in the space: real Apache text, plus a clause that quietly removes the freedoms. Read past the badge.
